macro_summary: Markets face a dense binary-event cluster July 28–30: FOMC rate decision (July 29) carries ~25–30% market-implied probability of a surprise 25bp hike, immediately followed by MSFT and META earnings (July 29 after close) and AAPL (July 30 after close). The Nasdaq sold off 2.15% on July 23 — its worst mega-cap session since the April 2025 tariff rout — as Alphabet and Tesla misses dragged the tape lower despite no structural deterioration in AI demand. Brent crude above $100 and elevated geopolitical risk add a macro tail. The consensus view across overnight screens is that this selloff has created pre-earnings dislocation in high-quality AI names, while the stacked binary risk justifies defined-risk structures (spreads, straddles) over outright directional exposure.
screening_note: 6 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. All 6 qualifying ideas met or exceeded threshold. Ideas ranked by appearance count descending.
key_events: FOMC rate decision — July 29, 2026 (~25–30% market-implied hike probability)
MSFT Q2 FY2026 earnings — July 29, 2026 after close (same day as Fed)
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings — July 30, 2026 after close
Nasdaq-100 fell 2.15% on July 23 (Alphabet/Tesla contagion); Brent crude >$100

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 6 of 6 overnight screens at unanimous high conviction — the strongest consensus signal in this digest. The stock sits near its cheapest forward P/E since 2018 (~21–22x NTM EPS) with 95% of analysts rating Buy and a median price target of ~$550–590 versus a last close near $394, implying 40–56% upside. The July 23 Nasdaq selloff created an incremental pre-earnings dip entry. A defined-risk call spread is the preferred structure given (a) elevated IV into the print and (b) same-day Fed rate decision on July 29 adding a hawkish-surprise tail.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2026 earnings report — July 29, 2026 after market close (same day as FOMC rate decision)
ideas.edge: Analyst median PT ~$550 vs. last close ~$394 — a ~40% gap; forward P/E ~21–22x, cheapest since 2018; consensus EPS $4.24 (+16.3% YoY), revenue $87.6B (+14.6% YoY); hyperscalers targeting $600B+ AI capex in 2026 directly supports Azure growth
ideas.bull_case: Strong Azure AI revenue beat resets sentiment on the entire Mag-7 cohort; Fed holds rates and the double catalyst (earnings + dovish hold) sparks a sharp gap-up recovery toward $490–550 analyst targets.
ideas.bear_case: Hawkish Fed surprise on July 29 compresses multiples before the earnings reaction is processed; any Azure growth deceleration below ~30% YoY risks a sell-the-news gap-down despite cheap valuation.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in all 6 of 6 overnight screens at unanimous high conviction. The stock was pulled down by GOOGL/Tesla contagion on July 23 despite a fundamentally different — and less capex-exposed — services/hardware business mix. Citi raised its PT to $365 (July 13) and HSBC upgraded to Buy with a $366 PT (July 17), both citing AI market-share gains, creating a >20% gap to last close near $334. Consensus expects EPS of $1.89 (+20.5% YoY) and revenue of $108.6B (+15.5% YoY) — a defined-risk call spread into the July 30 print captures the setup while bounding downside in a hawkish-rate environment.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings report — July 30, 2026 after market close (one day after FOMC decision and MSFT print)
ideas.edge: Dual upgrades: Citi PT $365 (Jul 13) and HSBC Buy/PT $366 (Jul 17) vs. last close ~$334 — a >20% gap; consensus EPS $1.89 (+20.5% YoY), revenue $108.6B (+15.5% YoY); AAPL less capex-exposed than hyperscalers, reducing GOOGL-contagion risk
ideas.bull_case: Services revenue and AI iPhone attach-rate beat estimates; post-FOMC relief rally combines with strong print to close the gap toward $365–366 analyst targets in a single session.
ideas.bear_case: If the Fed hikes on July 29, risk-off sentiment compounds into the July 30 print; any softness in Greater China hardware sales or slower-than-expected Apple Intelligence adoption could disappoint elevated analyst expectations.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 of 6 overnight screens at unanimous high conviction — the only pure-stock recommendation in the digest. Listed on Nasdaq on July 10 at $149, it is NVIDIA's primary HBM memory supplier and a direct proxy for the AI infrastructure buildout. July 29 marks its first-ever US earnings event, a historically strong catalyst for newly listed names still in price discovery. Analyst consensus target of $281.67 implies ~70% upside from the last close of ~$165; server DRAM prices are forecast to rise 18% in Q3 2026; and management guided that the global HBM shortage persists beyond 2030. Note: options availability is uncertain for this newly listed ADR — stock purchase is the primary execution route; size for post-IPO volatility.
ideas.catalyst: First Nasdaq earnings report as SKHY — July 29, 2026; secondary read-through from NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings on August 26, 2026
ideas.edge: Consensus 12-month PT $281.67 vs. last close ~$165 — ~70% upside; IPO priced at $149 (stock still above floor); net income +108% YoY, revenue +41% YoY; server DRAM prices forecast +18% in Q3 2026; dominant HBM supplier to NVDA with shortage persisting beyond 2030
ideas.bull_case: First US earnings beat with explicit HBM demand commentary triggers institutional accumulation; rising DRAM pricing guidance narrows the gap toward the $281 consensus target over 3–6 months as the ADR gains analyst coverage breadth.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO lock-up dynamics and thin ADR liquidity amplify downside on any earnings miss or management guidance disappointment; a hawkish Fed on July 29 could compress the entire semiconductor complex the same day SKHY reports.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens with mixed high/medium conviction. The stock pulled back ~2% on July 23 in sympathy with Alphabet/Tesla despite no NVDA-specific negative — hyperscaler capex of $600B+ in 2026 and GOOGL's $205B commitment are demand tailwinds, not headwinds, for GPU sales. Q1 FY2027 revenue came in at $81.6B (+85% YoY) with data center at $75.2B; the August 26 earnings print gives a 33-day runway for the AI narrative to reassert. A longer-dated call spread (30–45 DTE) is the preferred structure to define risk into both the near-term MSFT/AAPL AI-capex read-through (July 29–30) and the company's own earnings event.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings — August 26, 2026 after market close; near-term read-through from MSFT Azure and AAPL AI capex guidance July 29–30
ideas.edge: Contagion-driven ~2% pullback on July 23 despite no NVDA-specific negative; hyperscaler AI capex $600B+ in 2026 directly supports GPU demand; Q1 FY2027 data center revenue $75.2B; Mizuho names NVDA top semi pick; global chip sales +104% YoY (May 2026)
ideas.bear_case: Elevated implied volatility inflates option premiums over a 33-day hold; any export control escalation or hyperscaler capex pause commentary from MSFT/AAPL earnings could re-pressure the stock before the August 26 print.
ideas.bull_case: MSFT/AAPL earnings (July 29–30) include strong Azure AI and Apple Intelligence capex commentary, directly validating GPU demand and catalyzing a recovery toward prior highs well before NVDA's own August 26 report.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 3 of 6 screens as the preferred macro event-risk vehicle. The Nasdaq-100 has already fallen ~1.9–2.15% on July 23 and faces its most concentrated binary-event window of the year: FOMC rate decision July 29 (with ~25% hike probability), MSFT earnings same day, META same day, and AAPL the next morning. A put spread (30–45 DTE) or straddle/strangle centered on the July 29–30 window captures outsized directional moves in either direction while defining max loss at a time when outright short risk is high if all three catalysts resolve positively.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision — July 29, 2026 + MSFT/META earnings July 29 + AAPL earnings July 30 (triple binary 48-hour window)
ideas.edge: Nasdaq-100 already down ~2.15% from July 23 Alphabet/Tesla contagion; ~25% CME FedWatch hike probability not fully priced in recent price action; worst mega-cap session since April 2025 tariff rout — elevated realized vol supports option structure value
ideas.bull_case: Fed holds rates and all three mega-caps beat — QQQ snaps back sharply from an oversold level, making a call spread or straddle long-leg profitable; the selloff proves to be a sentiment overreaction rather than a fundamental repricing.
ideas.bear_case: Options premiums are already elevated from realized vol spike — straddle buyers face accelerated time decay if the FOMC and earnings deliver mixed, in-line results rather than a large directional move; IV crush post-event can erode gains even on a correct directional call.

ideas.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
ideas.thesis: SPY appeared in 2 of 6 screens — at the minimum qualifying threshold — primarily as a portfolio-level hedge rather than a directional trade. The S&P 500 fell 1.21% on July 23 with Brent above $100 and geopolitical risk elevated. A near-dated put spread expiring around August 1 captures the July 29 triple catalyst (Fed + MSFT + META) at a defined and manageable cost. The ~25–30% market-implied hike probability creates enough tail risk to justify a hedge overlay even if the base case is a Fed hold and earnings beats.
ideas.catalyst: Fed rate decision — July 29, 2026 (25–30% market-implied probability of a 25bp hike, same day as MSFT and META earnings)
ideas.edge: S&P 500 already –1.21% on July 23; ~25–30% hike probability creates asymmetric put-spread payoff if realized — 70–75% of the time the hedge expires worthless at low cost, but 25–30% of the time provides meaningful portfolio protection at a defined premium outlay
ideas.bull_case: Fed holds with a neutral tone, mega-cap earnings beats absorb the macro uncertainty, and SPY recovers — put spread expires with a small, defined loss that functioned as low-cost insurance on an already-oversold index.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish surprise or guidance disappointment from two or more mega-caps in a 48-hour window triggers a multi-percent SPY drawdown that exceeds the spread's protection range, leaving partial unhedged exposure.
